content.question:
Fill each of the numbered blanks in the following passage. Use only one word in each space.

One of the (1) ___ problems with holidays, (2) ___ from the usual travel complications and accommodation difficulties, (3) ___ the expectations people have of (4) ___. When we go on holiday we expect to leave all the stresses and strains of our (5) ___ lives behind us. We imagine we will be (6) ___ to escape to such a (7) ___ that we even tend to believe, consciously or not, that we can leave our (8) ___ personalities behind and become completely different people. The (9) ___ business-person, tense, preoccupied, short-tempered, (10) ___ to relax, envisages herself/himself becoming, from the moment of locking the office door, a radically different (11) ___ of person: carefree, good-humoured, ready to relax and enjoy whatever adventures present themselves. (12) ___ practice, we take ourselves with us (13) ___ we go, and the personality that is (14) ___ over years of stress and tension is almost impossible to shake off at a moment's (15) ___. It is no wonder so many holidays are a disappointment, no (16) ___ how smoothly they go or how lovely the weather is. In (17) ___, the innumerable problems that (18) ___ up during the average holiday are probably a welcome (19) ___ from the nagging feeling that we are not enjoying ourselves as much (20) ___ we should.
content.answer: biggest / greatest / worst ; apart ; is ; them ; daily / everyday ; able ; degree ; own ; average ; unable ; sort / kind / type ; In ; wherever ; formed ; notice ; matter ; fact ; crop / build ; distraction / change ; as

content.question:
Use only one word to fill in each of the numbered blanks.

Until half a century (21) ___, the basic physical structure you were born with was, (22) ___ you suffered an accident, the one you died with. Apart (23) ___ normal wear and tear or the possibility of a broken nose or other disfigurement, you (24) ___ not expect to change your appearance drastically. However, people have not always been (25) ___ with their lot. Fortunes have been (26) ___ by selling corsets, wigs and miraculous wrinkle removers. Magazines have been full of (27) ___ on how to disguise the shape of your face by (28) ___ blushers and foundations (29) ___ the day these products were invented. Then fresh hope appeared in the (30) ___ of cosmetic surgery. Although general practitioners warn their patients not to (31) ___ their lives to be transformed when they (32) ___ their noses remodelled or their ears tucked back, nevertheless there is a constant supply (33) ___ willing victims streaming (34) ___ the plush clinics of the knife-wielders. It is the people in the public (35) ___ who normally have the most drastic changes made. However, many ordinary people dream of looking dramatically (36) ___, save for years to (37) ___ their dream come true, and are willing to (38) ___ up with the inevitable swelling, scarring and black eyes. While some people sell stories to the tabloid press about (39) ___ their lives have changed, many others are sadly (40) ___ with the results.
content.answer: ago ; unless ; from ; could / did ; satisfied / contented / happy ; made ; advice ; applying / using ; since ; shape / form / guise ; expect ; have ; of ; into ; eye ; different ; make ; put ; how ; disappointed
